user_request: Explore the partitioning of groundwater and soil moisture and their drivers and impacts in the Naches sub-watershed using ELM, and validate against observations.
experiment_focus: Characterize how water partitions between soil moisture, sub-surface drainage/recharge, and runoff across the Naches sub-watershed; identify the drivers (elevation, soil, vegetation) of that partitioning; and validate against available observations.

domain.name: Naches River sub-watershed
domain.description: Largest tributary of the Yakima River. Drains the east slope of the Cascade Range, east of Mt Rainier and NE of Mt Adams. Strong vertical ecohydrologic gradient: subalpine / mixed-conifer headwaters -> ponderosa pine -> shrub-steppe near the mouth.

bbox.source: USGS WBD via terrain MCP resolve_watershed (real HUC 17030002 extent)

elevation_range_m.note: Published ~1070 ft at the mouth to ~8000 ft in the headwaters. The terrain MCP elevation_summary samples 3DEP across the bbox for stratification bands at run time.

heterogeneity.elevation_relief: High, ~2100 m span — primary driver candidate.
heterogeneity.soil: Spatially variable. SSURGO is available POINT-WISE only; no gridded/polygon coverage is wired.
heterogeneity.land_cover: Gradient with elevation: shrub-steppe (low) -> ponderosa pine (mid) -> mixed conifer / subalpine (high).
heterogeneity.where_partitioning_concentrates: Valley bottoms / riparian corridors and the rain-snow transition zone.

climate_baseline.note: Precip/temp NOT fetched in this stubbed brief. A strong orographic gradient is expected (wet headwaters, dry mouth) but should come from data, not assumption.

soil_profile.note: Point-only today. Watershed-scale heterogeneity requires gridded/polygon soil — a capability gap.

observations_available.source: USGS NWIS gages (live; bbox-queryable with the existing usgs-water MCP today)
observations_available.role: Integrated water-balance constraint at the outlet / sub-basins.

observations_available.source: Fan et al. 2013 well compilation (static dataset; NOT yet wired)
observations_available.in_domain: likely present (US-wide compilation built from USGS + state well records)

observations_missing.in_domain: NONE. Nearest WA towers (US-Wrc Wind River Crane, US-xWR NEON WREF) are west-slope wet Douglas-fir/hemlock — wrong side of the Cascade crest, not climatically analogous.
observations_missing.analog_option: Metolius ponderosa cluster (US-Me family), central OR — east-Cascades semi-arid. Usable for PROCESS / parameterization validation only, NOT co-located validation.
observations_missing.status: gap — acceptable to flag and proceed without it

available_capabilities: Watershed boundary + bbox + area via terrain MCP resolve_watershed (USGS WBD).
3DEP elevation: point queries + bbox grid sampling + elevation bands via terrain MCP (get_elevation, sample_elevation_grid, elevation_summary).
Observed water-table depth: USGS groundwater wells in a bbox + depth-to-water series via usgs_water MCP (get_groundwater_sites, get_water_table_depth). ~50+ GW wells in the Naches bbox.
Fan (2013) equilibrium water-table depth via fan_wtd MCP (get_fan_wtd, sample_fan_wtd) — LIVE: NAMERICA annual-mean tile loaded; returns positive depth_to_water_m below land surface.
known_data_gaps: Soil is point-only; no gridded/polygon heterogeneity (SSURGO point query only).
FLUXNET soil-moisture not ingested; no in-domain tower (analog: Metolius US-Me).

_meta.purpose: Hand-written domain brief for the planner capability probe (tools/probe_planner.py). Simulates what an upgraded domain-mode reception agent would produce. NOT from live MCP. The schema it converges on becomes reception's output spec.
